source: Remediation sequence C1-C6 (fresh-eyes audit 2026-06-06); filed for later manual implementation. See docs/PROJECT_MAP.md and the approved plan.
title: Trim demo-ware and gate the tool-count claim (C5)
what_failed: The 64-tool claim overstates hardened capability: tools_wakeword.py and tools_scheduler.py export ZERO ToolSpecs (dead weight); video/computer-use/browser/faces/STT are mock-tested only. The count is a pitch feature and a maintenance liability.
where_failed: tools/tools_wakeword.py, tools/tools_scheduler.py (no TOOLS export); docs claim '64 tools' flatly.
why_likely: Phase-8 feature burst; surface area grew faster than hardening.
migration_plan: 1. Archive zero-ToolSpec modules (tools_wakeword.py, tools_scheduler.py) to docs/_archive/ (never delete).
2. Mark mock-only tools ◐ experimental wherever tools are listed (PI.md/README/ABOUT).
3. Define the public 'tool count' as tools with >=1 behavioral test (ties to T-166); state it honestly.
implementation_notes: Smart and simple: archive dead weight, label the rest honestly. Build no new tooling - just stop overclaiming.
analysis: C5: for the 'sellable surface' goal, a smaller honestly-hardened toolset beats a big mock-tested one.
